no: Must install BEFORE Sword Coast Stratagems. ToF components use FORBID_COMPONENT checks for SCS 5900 (Initialise AI). Many components skipped if SCS installs first. Extensive overlap with SCS spell tweaks, cdtweaks, HouseTweaks, ArtisansKitpack, and other tweak mods. Many SCS components have ToF equivalents — use one set per feature. INSTALL FIXES APPLIED (all 6 SFO copies: ToF, SCS, IWDification, MiH FR/SP/Metamod): 1) disjunctive_substitution.tph — exclusion list broadened to BD.* + A7[#!].* + C#AJAN regexp. Skips SoD companion scripts, Golem Construction scripts, and Ajantis BCS that can't round-trip decompile/recompile. 2) alter_script.tph — PATCH_RERAISE replaced with error recovery. On BCS round-trip failure, saves/restores original BCS unchanged instead of aborting the entire component install. Mirrors WeiDU core's decompile_and_patch_context behavior. 3) cd_arcane_post.tpa — copies 768-byte idpro313.pro from copyover/ to override before COPY_EXISTING patch (vanilla file is 512 bytes, writes at offset 0x214 fail without this). 4) ui_externalize_hlas.tph — REPLACE_EVALUATE regex narrowed to [0-9]+ for numeric-only first arg, with pass-through for non-numeric args. Compatible with vanilla, LeUI, and Infinity UI. 5) ui_add_subraces.tph — IS_AN_INT guard added to column comparison in subrace_clab_patch. Prevents crash on HLA tables with non-numeric column headers (e.g. SKYEHL.2da from Skitia NPCs). Users applying manually: fix files in all 6 SFO copies listed above. Fixes 1-2 are in sfo/ or sfo2e/ subdirectories. Fix 3 is in dw_talents/iwdspells/lib/. Fixes 4-5 are in dw_talents/sfo/lua/. Automated fixes: eet-mod-forge-patches components #1-3.

ki.pattern: ERROR Installing \[Install Druid kits\]|ERROR Installing \[Install all spell tweaks\]|ERROR Installing \[Introduce new races and subraces\]|ERROR Installing \[Make Barbarian Into a Class\]|ERROR Installing \[Revised shapeshift powers\]|ERROR Installing \[New blackguard kits\]|ERROR Installing \[Allow druids to multiclass\]|ERROR Installing \[Characters choose minor new abilities\]|ERROR Installing \[Revised and expanded high-level abilities\]|ERROR Installing \[Improved NPC customisation\]|ERROR Installing \[Revised Weapon Proficiency\]
ki.description: Multiple ToF components fail due to unpatched SFO bugs: (1) BCS round-trip failures in ALTER_SCRIPT_BLOCK — DECOMPILE_BCS_TO_BAF has no PATCH_TRY, triggered by Turnabout mod's large scripts (d0t#alia.bcs 112KB, d0t#dyna.bcs 333KB). (2) idpro313.pro size mismatch crashes patches (fix #2). (3) Non-numeric HLA column headers crash subrace CLAB patching (fix #3). (4) Large batches (25+ components) segfault from SFO memory pressure, corrupting TLK/override state on retry. Also cascading if ArtisansKitpack's Invalid_argument crash left corrupted BCS files in override.
ki.workaround: Apply infinity-mod-runner patches (#1-3) to all 6 SFO copies. Use small batch sizes (3) for dw_talents to prevent segfault cascades. Without patches, cn:20000/40150 fail with Turnabout installed; without small batches, cn:2000/2020 fail on retry after segfault.

ki.description: Late-sequence dw_talents batches can cascade into slowdowns lasting tens of minutes to multiple hours per batch. Observed: session 3e3996c9 had batch 389 run 1.2h before user abort; session c623ff72 saw batches 393→398 grow 4m→6m→9m→8m→9m→51m in succession before cancellation. Pattern appears when ToF is installed alongside Faiths and Powers, Might & Guile, Song and Silence, Rogue Rebalancing, Tome and Blood. Likely cause: SFO memory pressure accumulating across batches, with specific late components doing heavy CLAB/spell-table patching (cn:41000 Rebalanced Kits, cn:40950 Dragon Disciples, HLA revisions, or UI externalization pass).
ki.workaround: If a dw_talents batch exceeds ~10 minutes, consider aborting and resuming with smaller batch sizes, or split the install — complete dw_talents standalone before adding other heavy kit/spell mods. The 'Never abort' toggle is NOT appropriate here; the process genuinely slows over time rather than hanging outright. The orchestrator will now emit a 'slow batch' warning at 10min to prompt the user.
ki.source: Session references extracted from description on 2026-04-21 during Phase 2.3 validator rollout: session 3e3996c9; session c623ff72

ki.pattern: Installing \[Revised and expanded high-level abilities system\]|60200.*(Revised|HLA)|making feat EMPOWERED_MONSTER_SUMMONING
ki.description: Component #60200 (Revised and expanded HLA system) is the single slowest component in dw_talents — typically 2-3 hours wall clock on an EET megainstall. 98% of that time is consumed by ONE feat: EMPOWERED_MONSTER_SUMMONING. The feat calls SFO's iter_resource on each of 11 wizard summoning spells (Monster Summoning 1-7, Carrion, Wyvern Call, Spider Spawn), which recursively walks every creature, script, spell, and item each spell references. Each walked BCS triggers COPY_EXISTING + DECOMPILE_BCS_TO_BAF. Observed in session 6693001a: 237,330 'Copying and patching 1 file ...' lines from this feat alone, out of 242,225 for the entire component. The recursive graph walk has no cross-call caching, so each of the 11 spells re-walks the shared summoning subgraph from scratch. Upstream (hla_spec_summons.tph line 14) also contains a duplicate %WIZARD_MONSTER_SUMMONING_1% entry (typo), doubling work for one of the 11 spells.
ki.workaround: Two infinity-mod-runner patches address this. Apply BOTH for the stacked effect. (1) Patch #58 (_eetmr_dedupe_ms1_v1) removes the duplicate WIZARD_MONSTER_SUMMONING_1 from the spell_list, saving ~15-20 min standalone. (2) Patch #59 (_eetmr_graph_accumulator_v1) makes the 11 iter_resource calls share visited-node state across the spell_list loop, so each subsequent call short-circuits already-walked subgraphs. Estimated combined savings: 60-90% of the 3h wall clock — component should complete in 20-60 min instead. Patch #59 modifies 3 files (lib_math.tph adds opt-in keep_visited param, lib_iter.tph forwards it, hla_spec_summons.tph uses keep_visited=1 with a single outer clear). The extra param is backward-compatible (default preserves legacy behaviour). Only the dw_talents/sfo/ copy is patched — other mods' SFO copies untouched. Also benefits EMPOWERED_ANIMAL/UNDEAD/TOTEMIC_SUMMONING since they route through the same empowered_creature_setup.
ki.source: Session references extracted from description on 2026-04-21 during Phase 2.3 validator rollout: session 6693001a

ki.pattern: WARNING: Cannot uninstall STRING_SET #[0-9]+, out of range
ki.description: When a dw_talents component fails mid-install, the rollback tries to uninstall STRING_SETs that were never fully registered. These warnings are cosmetic — the rollback still completes. Accompanies any dw_talents install failure.
ki.workaround: Ignore; the actual error is whichever ERROR line precedes the rollback.

new: Natural gift for magic of unknown origin. Bonus spell slots and arcane power that grows with experience.
Touched by the Plane of Shadow. Gains shadow magic abilities, stealth bonuses, and resistance to darkness.
Touched by capricious fey nature. Gains charm abilities, woodland stride, and fey-themed innate powers.
Blessed by celestial power. Gains holy resistance, healing abilities, and radiant damage bonuses.
Tainted by fiendish influence. Gains fire resistance, dark abilities, and infernal innate powers.
Tainted by the grave. Gains undead resistance, necromantic abilities, and death-themed powers.
Born in the heart of a storm. Gains lightning abilities, electrical resistance, and storm-themed powers.
Descended from efreeti of the Plane of Fire. Gains fire abilities, heat resistance, and flame powers.
Ice runs in these veins. Gains cold abilities, frost resistance, and winter-themed innate powers.
Chromatic red dragon heritage. Fire breath weapon, fire resistance, AC and HP bonuses.
Chromatic blue dragon heritage. Lightning breath weapon, electrical resistance, AC and HP bonuses.
Chromatic green dragon heritage. Acid breath weapon, acid resistance, AC and HP bonuses.
Chromatic black dragon heritage. Acid breath weapon, acid resistance, AC and HP bonuses.
Specialist wizard focusing on fire magic. Extra fire spell slot per level. Cannot cast water/ice spells.
Specialist wizard focusing on water magic. Extra water spell slot per level. Cannot cast fire spells.
Specialist wizard focusing on air magic. Extra air spell slot per level. Cannot cast earth spells.
Specialist wizard focusing on earth magic. Extra earth spell slot per level. Cannot cast air spells.
Specialist wizard focusing on pure magical force. Uses force missiles, walls, and shields.
Combat abjurer combining protective magic with martial weapon training.
Combat conjurer combining summoning magic with martial weapon training.
Combat invoker combining offensive magic with martial weapon training.
Combat transmuter combining alteration magic with martial weapon training.
Sorcerer whose innate magic is tied to deep rage. Can enter a magical frenzy combining arcane and fury.
Dwarven priest-warrior of the chief dwarven deity. Combines martial prowess with divine forge magic.
Dwarven smith-priest of Moradin. Expert in crafting and divine forge magic.
War priest who channels Tempus through berserker fury. Combines rage with divine battle magic.
Priest of the storm god. Commands lightning and destructive weather magic.
Warrior-priest of the moon goddess. Combines swordplay with lunar divine magic.
Priest trained to counter arcane magic. Dispel Magic at enhanced power, magic resistance.
Dark warrior-priest of the god of tyranny. Fear aura, dark divine magic.
Priest of the Son of Bane. Commands fear and dark divine power.
Holy warrior of the Morninglord. Bonus vs undead, radiant abilities.
Priest-knight of the god of justice. Detects evil, smites the unjust.
Defensive priest of the Watcher. Protection aura, enhanced vigilance abilities.
Priest of the god of thieves. Stealth abilities combined with shadow divine magic.
Shadow priest of Mask. Enhanced stealth, shadow magic, and divine thievery.
Knowledge priest of the god of invention. Enhanced lore, identification, and divination.
Divination specialist priest of Oghma. Scrying and knowledge-seeking divine magic.
Cleric of the Crying God. Absorbs pain of allies, enhanced healing.
Protective priest of Ilmater. Specializes in abjuration and shielding divine magic.
Priest of Lady Luck. Luck-based abilities, fortune favors in combat and saves.
Priest of the dead god of murder. Assassination abilities and death magic.
Cleric of the Firelord. Fire immunity and flame divine magic.
Priest of the goddess of magic. Combines arcane and divine spellcasting.
Divine caster chosen by a deity. Casts divine spells spontaneously like a sorcerer.

Blackguard who made an unholy pact with Asmodeus. Gains infernal powers and fiendish abilities.

co.no: INSTALL FIX APPLIED: cd_arcane_post.tpa — vanilla idpro313.pro is 512 bytes (type 2 missile) but ToF writes at offsets 0x214/0x21a which require 768-byte type 3 (area effect) format. Fix: copy ToF's own 768-byte idpro313.pro from copyover/WIZARD_LANCE_OF_DISRUPTION/ to override BEFORE the COPY_EXISTING patch. Users must apply this fix in dw_talents/iwdspells/lib/cd_arcane_post.tpa: add 'COPY ~%%MOD_FOLDER%%/%%component_loc%%/copyover/WIZARD_LANCE_OF_DISRUPTION/idpro313.pro~ ~override~' before the COPY_EXISTING ~idpro313.pro~ line. Automated fix: eet-mod-forge-patches component #3.

advisories.reason: Component-level overlaps — choose carefully if using both. ToF cn:41000 vs AK cn:5100 (Shapeshifter): DESTRUCTIVE — both replace CLABDR03.2DA, pick one. ToF cn:41000 vs AK cn:1003 (Berserker): ToF edits SPCL321 (Rage) but AK replaces the CLAB entirely — ToF's changes become dead code. ToF cn:41000 vs AK cn:1006 (Wizard Slayer): both modify MR progression, may stack. ToF cn:81100 vs AK cn:1100 (Fighter Overhaul): AK's auto-specialization spell uses SPLPROT checks that may silently fail under ToF's Lua proficiency system. AK cn:1004 (Kensai): safe. AK Assassin/Blackguard poison breaks ToF feat system. Neither detects the other. Install AK before ToF.
